title: Don't Drive Me Away
content:
"Don't drive me away,
        But hear what I say:
        Bad men want the gold;
        They will steal it to-night,
        And you must take flight;
        So be quiet and busy and bold."

        "Slip away with me,
        And you will see
        What a wise little thing am I;
        For the road I show
        No man can know,
        Since it's up in the pathless sky."
meaning:
About: Louisa May Alcott was an American novelist. 
She is best known for the novel Little Women, published in 1868. This novel is loosely based on her childhood experiences with her three sisters.
